channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump floats gutting the IRS, moving agents to the border armed with guns
item.description: President Donald Trump floated moving the thousands of IRS agents hired under the Biden administration to the southern border to help patrol the area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As Hegseth takes charge at the Pentagon, here's what changes could be in store
item.description: There will be a lot on the plate for new Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth when he steps into his office on the Pentagon’s third floor E Ring.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor And Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ramaswamy's expected run for Ohio governor would test experienced Republicans and tradition
item.description: Fresh off of his unexpected departure from President Donald Trump's Department of Government Efficiency, Vivek Ramaswamy has set his sights on becoming governor of Ohio, his home state.
item.dc:creator: Julie Carr Smyth And Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democrats are splintered on immigration and how to respond to Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When Donald Trump took office in 2017 with promises to crack down on immigration, he was met by widespread protests that filled churches, airports and union halls as Democratic lawmaker vowed to fight the new Republican president at every turn.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's whirlwind week back at the White House
item.description: President Trump swept into the White House with a whirlwind of a first week, quickly making good on a number of campaign promises while embracing the trappings of the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump touts 'no-tax-on-tips' plan in Nevada victory lap
item.description: When Nevada flipped red in November, Trump became the first GOP presidential candidate to win the key swing state since President George W. Bush in 2004.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Florida Republican urges Trump to spare some migrants under deportation plan
item.description: Salazar, a daughter of Cuban exiles, represents Florida's 27th Congressional District which is home to a number of Venezuelans and Cubans.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: White House fires some independent inspectors general
item.description: NewsNation partner The Hill reported that Trump has ousted around 17 federal watchdogs within various departments
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats and voting groups say a bid to toss out North Carolina ballots is an attack on democracy
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Copland Rudolph cast a ballot in the November election, just as she has for years, with her vote counting on a long list of North Carolina contests that were settled soon after.
item.dc:creator: BY GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's 5 biggest moves on immigration so far
item.description: President Trump released a flurry of immigration actions this week, signing a series of orders targeting the border and ramping up enforcement. While Trump has long pledged to address illegal immigration, many of his actions targeted longstanding legal pathways.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: China tells Rubio to behave himself in veiled warning
item.description: The short phrase seemed aimed at Rubio's vocal criticism of China and its human rights record when he was a U.S. senator.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: McConnell voted no on Hegseth as Pentagon head, forcing Vance to cast tiebreaker
item.description: Sen. Mitch McConnell was one of three Republicans to vote against Pete Hegseth, who was narrowly confirmed as President Donald Trump's defense secretary.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Dakota’s overturned abortion ban won’t be in effect during appeal, court rules
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — North Dakota’s abortion ban will not be enforced while the state appeals an earlier decision that found it unconstitutional, the state's highest court ruled Friday. That appeal has yet to fully play out in the state Supreme Court after a judge struck down the law in September.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump ‘very surprised’ by Murkowski, Collins votes against Hegseth
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday that he wasn't expecting Republican Sens. Lisa Murkowski (Alaska) and Susan Collins (Maine) to vote against his controversial nominee for secretary of Defense, Army veteran and former Fox News host Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Measure to make California an independent country cleared to gather signatures
item.description: Another ballot measure asking whether California should be its own country is cleared to start gathering signatures, the California Secretary of State announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's Cabinet picks are set for Senate hearings. Here's the schedule
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nominations process for President Donald Trump's appointees is picking up pace, with some confirmed by the Senate and hearings scheduled for more of his Cabinet picks. Many nominees have met with senators individually.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: GOP leaders in debt limit quandary between Trump, fiscal hawks
item.description: Congressional GOP leaders are in a massive pickle as they weigh how to deal with a looming deadline to raise the debt ceiling. President Trump is pressuring Republicans to not allow Democrats to use the borrowing limit as a leverage point.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s Jan. 6 pardons divide House Republicans
item.description: President Trump’s decision to pardon hundreds of protesters who stormed the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021 — including many accused of assaulting police officers — is dividing House Republicans, with some lawmakers touting the day-one decree and others criticizing it as ill-advised.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance speaks at March for Life rally in Washington
item.description: A rally is scheduled for noon on the National Mall and we expect a string of Republicans to speak about their anti-abortion plans.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk tried to turn the salute controversy into a joke
item.description: The incident only lasted seconds, but it sparked what has become a global debate about how to interpret what Musk did. Then Musk started posting Nazi-themed jokes.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block and Shannon Bond, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota justices tell lawmakers to work out control of ‘dysfunctional’ House
item.description: The Minnesota Supreme Court on Friday agreed with DFLers that it takes 68 votes to conduct House business, but the opinion didn’t come with a road map on how the House should proceed from here.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Walz proposes $887 million bonding bill for infrastructure projects, not worried about Capitol impasse
item.description: Using the breached Rapidan Dam as a backdrop Gov. Tim Walz pitched his $887 million bonding bill. As well as funding for infrastructure, Walz wants to fund a new state patrol headquarters, and address housing and environmental needs.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Top Minnesota attorney: Birthright citizenship suit against Trump just the beginning
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order redefining birthright citizenship two days after Minnesota joined 21 other states in a lawsuit to challenge it. Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison discussed his strategy.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Megan Burks, MPR News

item.title: US active duty troops beginning to arrive in Texas, San Diego to support border security
item.description: Active duty military troops will begin arriving in El Paso, Texas, and San Diego on Thursday evening, in what defense officials said is the first batch of the new forces being deployed to secure the southern border.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pardons anti-abortion activists who blockaded clinic entrances
item.description: President Donald Trump announced Thursday he would pardon anti-abortion activists convicted of blockading abortion clinic entrances.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: State Supreme Court weighs what to do about ‘dysfunctional’ Minnesota House
item.description: The high court heard oral arguments Thursday over the temporary control of the Minnesota House. “This is a highly unusual situation we find ourselves in,” Justice Anne McKeig said during early questioning of attorneys.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump order halting refugee resettlement disrupting Minnesota agencies’ efforts
item.description: Officials with refugee resettlement agencies in Minnesota say Trump’s executive order halting the Refugee Admissions Program is disrupting efforts to bring people facing strife in their home countries to the U.S. and to the state.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court won’t take up fight over Pennsylvania mail ballot date requirement
item.description: The Supreme Court said Tuesday it won’t hear civil rights groups’ appeal seeking to invalidate Pennsylvania's dating requirement for mail ballots, which has invalidated thousands of votes each recent election and become a central legal fight over voting in the key swing state.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Trump fires 4 presidential appointees, including his former Iran envoy
item.description: President Donald announced the firing of four high-profile presidential appointees just after midnight Tuesday, including a top envoy to Iran during his first term and retired Gen. Mark Milley, who Trump tapped as Joint Chiefs chairman in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Trump to issue executive orders defining sexes, ending DEI practices in government
item.description: President-elect Trump intends to issue executive orders on his first day in office that will recognize only two sexes, male and female, and aim to end diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) practices in the federal government, according to incoming White House officials.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5
